title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot visits Ariadne Oliver at her home, where he is introduced to her friend, Judith Butler. Judith, described as having golden hair and sea-green eyes, originally met Ariadne in Greece after saving her from drowning. While Miranda serves them tea, the conversation shifts to the murder of Joyce Reynolds. Judith questions the motive behind the crime, wondering why Joyce—who was known for being a habitual liar—would suddenly claim to have witnessed a murder. Poirot confirms that Joyce was indeed murdered.

The group discusses biblical stories from the Apocrypha, specifically the tale of Judith and Holofernes and the story of Jael, who killed Sisera by driving a nail into his head. Miranda expresses her distaste for such violence, remarking that if she had to deal with enemies, she would prefer to use a drug to allow them to die peacefully in their sleep.

After Miranda leaves to wash the dishes, Ariadne expresses her anxiety regarding the local atmosphere and the safety of children, urging Poirot to find the killer so the community can feel at ease. Poirot and Ariadne move into the garden to sit on a stone bench among the Elizabethan queen roses, where Ariadne asks Poirot for his impression of Judith.
